claim_text: Perceptual difference models are a class of psycho-physical models that aim to mimic how humans perceive image quality by modeling the human visual processing system.13–17

sub_claims.text: miao2008 is an example of a perceptual difference model and defines/uses a PDM (Case-PDM) as a psycho-physical model of the human visual processing system for image quality assessment of MR images.

evidence.snippet: Quantitative image quality evaluation of MR images using perceptual difference models

The authors are using a perceptual difference model (Case-PDM) to quantitatively evaluate image quality of the thousands of test images which can be created when optimizing fast magnetic resonance (MR) imaging strategies and reconstruction techniques.

To assess MR image reconstruction algorithms, we have used a perceptual difference model (Case-PDM) which mimics the functional anatomy of the visual pathway and contains components that model the optics and sensitivity of the retina, the spatial contrast sensitivity function, and the channels of spatial frequency found in the visual cortex.

Similar perceptual difference methods have been often applied to evaluate image compression. ... and perceptual metrics which attempt to model human visual processing based on psychophysical and physiological evidence (e.g., Refs. 38 and 39).

These mathematically based objective metrics do not utilize any information about viewing conditions and do not adapt to local image content, while these two issues play a major role in human perception of image quality.

In conclusion, considering all tests, Case-PDM scored the best of the seven models by providing high model-subject correlations and consistent prediction of detection thresholds for just perceptible differences across a variety of images and processing.

sub_claims.nuance: Paper title and body explicitly frame Case-PDM as a perceptual difference model that mimics the human visual pathway (retinal optics, CSF, cortical spatial-frequency channels) and groups it with perceptual metrics modeling 'human visual processing based on psychophysical and physiological evidence.' This squarely supports the manuscript's BACKGROUND claim that PDMs are psycho-physical models mimicking human visual processing for image-quality assessment.

section_checklist.section: II.B. Human subject correlation DSCQS experiment
II.C. Minimizing context effects FMT experiment
IV.C. Range of applicability of Case-PDM

attestation.indirect_attribution_check: Introduction notes the PDM structure 'is described in detail by Salem et al.' (ref 24) and a prior 2002 description, indicating Case-PDM has antecedents; however, miao2008 itself directly defines, uses, and characterizes a perceptual difference model in MR IQ evaluation, so it is a legitimate primary example for this BACKGROUND claim (not purely indirect).
attestation.out_of_context_check: Manuscript usage (PDMs as psycho-physical models mimicking the human visual system for IQ assessment) matches miao2008's framing of Case-PDM (mimics visual pathway: retinal optics, CSF, cortical channels) and its grouping with perceptual metrics 'based on psychophysical and physiological evidence' for image-quality assessment. No context mismatch.
